source: Sudan Federal Ministry of Health Expanded Programme on Immunization (EPI); WHO/UNICEF JRF 2024 country profile; WHO EMRO Sudan; UNICEF Sudan country pages
source_notes: Schedule composed from WHO/UNICEF JRF Sudan 2024 country profile, WHO EMRO Sudan EPI page, and the WHO Immunization Data portal for Sudan (SDN). WHO-aligned 6/10/14-week primary series. Rotavirus introduced 2011 (2-dose). IPV introduced into routine schedule. Yellow fever risk concentrated in southern/Blue Nile/Darfur areas — given as routine 9-month dose in YF-endemic states; not nationally routine. Meningococcal A (MenAfriVac) is given in meningitis-belt states.
verification_notes: Phase 1 seed marked needs_review on the country selector. Do NOT treat as clinically authoritative until row-level reconciliation against the current WHO/XMart 2024 JRF schedule and Sudan FMOH EPI bulletin is complete. Open items include: exact YF subnational targeting, current PCV product (PCV10 vs PCV13), HepB birth-dose coverage in conflict-affected states, MR2 vs MCV2 second-dose age, and TCV introduction status.
source_year_or_release_date: 2024 WHO/UNICEF JRF schedule data; seed first added 2026-04-25
scope: Routine childhood immunisation, birth to 72 months (6 years). Compulsory (Sudan EPI) and subnationally-routine antigens.
context_note: Source-country schedule. In cross-border reconciliation INTO Egypt, Sudan-EPI doses are the reliable signal on a Sudanese card. Yellow Fever and Meningococcal A doses (where given) are Sudan-routine but are not on the Egyptian schedule — they should be preserved on the record but not counted as 'missing' for Egypt.
key_differences_vs_egypt: Sudan uses WHO-aligned 6/10/14-week primary series; Egypt uses 2/4/6-month series
Sudan uses Pentavalent (DTPw-Hib-HepB, 5-in-1, no IPV); Egypt uses Hexavalent (6-in-1, includes IPV)
Sudan gives separate IPV at 14 weeks; Egypt's IPV is bundled in Hexavalent
Sudan gives Measles monovalent at 9 months and a second measles-containing dose at 18 months; Egypt gives MMR at 12 months and 18 months
Sudan has routine Rotavirus (Rota1 6w, Rota2 10w) in EPI; Egypt does not
Sudan has routine PCV at 6/10/14 weeks; Egypt PCV is recommended/private only
Sudan gives Yellow Fever at 9 months in YF-endemic states; Egypt does not (YF non-endemic)
Sudan gives Meningococcal A (MenAfriVac) at 9 months in meningitis-belt states; Egypt does not

doses.notes: At birth or as soon as possible after birth.
doses.verification_source: WHO/UNICEF JRF Sudan 2024; WHO EMRO Sudan

doses.notes: Birth dose (HBV0). Standalone monovalent HepB at birth. Coverage may be reduced in conflict-affected areas.
OPV0 at birth. Sudan uses bivalent OPV (bOPV) in routine schedule.
OPV1 at 6 weeks. Co-administered with Pentavalent, PCV, Rotavirus.
Penta1 at 6 weeks. 5-in-1 covering DTPw (whole-cell pertussis), Hib, HepB. Does NOT include IPV — IPV is given separately at 14 weeks.
PCV1 at 6 weeks. Sudan uses PCV (product label varies by procurement cycle — verify on card).
Rota1 at 6 weeks. 2-dose oral series (Rotarix-style). Series must be initiated by 15 weeks and completed by 24 months per WHO.
Rota2 at 10 weeks. Series complete after dose 2 (Rotarix).
Penta3 at 14 weeks. Primary DTPw-Hib-HepB series complete.
IPV1 at 14 weeks. Sudan introduced IPV into routine schedule for polio endgame (eradication strategy). Single fIPV dose at 14 weeks; second IPV dose under WHO consideration.
doses.verification_source: WHO/UNICEF JRF Sudan 2024; WHO Polio Eradication Initiative

doses.notes: PCV3 at 14 weeks. Primary PCV series complete.
Measles first dose (MCV1) at 9 months. Sudan public EPI delivers measles-monovalent at 9 months (not MMR).
Yellow Fever at 9 months in YF-endemic states (Darfur, Blue Nile, parts of South Kordofan). NOT given in YF-non-endemic states. WHO YF certificate is lifetime-valid.
doses.verification_source: WHO/UNICEF JRF Sudan 2024; WHO YF country list

doses.notes: MenAfriVac at 9 months in meningitis-belt states (parts of central/eastern Sudan).
doses.verification_source: WHO/UNICEF JRF Sudan 2024; WHO Meningitis Belt

doses.notes: Measles second dose (MCV2) at 18 months. Some cards may show 'MR2' (Measles-Rubella) — Sudan transitioned MCV2 to MR-containing in some states.

open_questions: Exact subnational targeting of Yellow Fever and MenA — verify against current Sudan FMOH EPI bulletins.
Current PCV product (PCV10 vs PCV13) — verify against most recent procurement cycle.
HepB birth-dose coverage in conflict-affected areas (Darfur, Khartoum 2023+ conflict) — coverage gaps likely.
MR (Measles-Rubella) introduction timeline at 9 vs 18 months — varies by state and year.
